UNESCO Criteria: (i) to represent a masterpiece of human creative genius;
Local Stone Name: Makrana Marble (Note: This is historically accurate but missing from the official UNESCO text)
Minerals: Calcite (chiefly), with minor quartz and mica
Structural Use: Main tomb structure, minarets, platforms, domes, and wall veneering.
Decorative Use: Exquisite octagonal marble lattice screens, pietra dura stone intarsia inlay (using precious/semi-precious stones), floral relief motifs, and Arabic calligraphic inscriptions.
Masonry Technique: Brick-in-lime mortar core veneered with dressed stone slices.
